blockers.title: Claim path allows non-standard members into the talent pipeline consent state
blockers.evidence: Stage 0 locks talent-pipeline visibility off for minor, ally, and restricted_unknown lanes (`02 Stage 0...md:182-190`), and the existing join/settings paths enforce `member_lane !== "standard"` (`convex/members.ts:537-550`). The new claim path instead allows `lane === "standard" || lane === "ally"` and writes the submitted pipeline value (`convex/members.ts:784-796`). The claim UI also always renders the pipeline checkbox (`src/portal/ClaimFlow.tsx:172-178`) despite the spec requiring it hidden for minors (`specs/claim-wave.spec.md:36-39`).
blockers.required_fix: Make claim consent follow the same rule as join/settings: only `standard` can produce `pipeline=true`; all other lanes get explicit false plus audit evidence. Hide/clear the checkbox for self-declared minors and male/ally claims, and add Convex regression tests.

blockers.title: Two-people-one-email conflict model is not enforced
blockers.evidence: Stage 0 says two people mapping to one email must route to manual review (`02 Stage 0...md:120-128`). The import script silently skips duplicate emails and continues live import (`scripts/import-members.py:121-123`) instead of failing or marking conflict. Backend claim lookup then collects all rows for the proven email and picks the first claimable row (`convex/members.ts:629-642`, `convex/members.ts:697-704`), which can expose one imported name and let the email holder claim the wrong row.
blockers.required_fix: Treat duplicate normalized emails as a hard import blocker or mark all affected imported rows `conflict`. Also make `getMyClaimCandidate`/`matchClaim` refuse ambiguous email matches without exposing row details. Add tests for duplicate-email imports and claims.

blockers.title: No-candidate sign-in falls through to the member dashboard
blockers.evidence: Acceptance criterion 2 says if no imported claim candidate exists, the current no-member message stays (`specs/claim-wave.spec.md:32-35`). `Dashboard` only handles `claim.state === "claimable"` and `"held"` (`src/portal/Dashboard.tsx:36-47`). When `claim` is still loading or resolves `null`, it falls through to the normal dashboard (`src/portal/Dashboard.tsx:109-213`), telling an unlinked authenticated user "You're a member" and showing profile/standing/opportunity tiles.
blockers.required_fix: Add explicit `me === null && claim === undefined` loading and `me === null && claim === null` no-member branches before the member dashboard. Preserve the existing no-member/support/sign-out guidance and do not render member tiles without a member row.

blockers.title: Legacy membership numbers are optional, so migrated certificates can get new numbers
blockers.evidence: The spec requires parsing WAIME-### legacy numbers and issuing migrated certificates with the legacy number (`specs/claim-wave.spec.md:24-31`, `specs/claim-wave.spec.md:54-55`). `scripts/import-members.py` allows `parse_legacy_number` to return `None` and still appends the row (`scripts/import-members.py:133-171`). The schema keeps `legacy_membership_number` optional (`convex/schema.ts:115`), `matchClaim` passes that optional value (`convex/members.ts:818-825`), and `issueMembershipCertificate` falls back to the new counter when it is undefined (`convex/lib/certificates.ts:49-66`).
blockers.required_fix: Make missing/unparseable legacy membership numbers fatal before live import, or refuse claim for those rows until manually resolved. Prefer making `legacy_membership_number` required for imported rows that can be claimed, and add a regression test proving migrated certificates cannot fall back to a new number.

non_blocking_notes: `./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. I did not run build or E2E because this review is read-only and those commands write artifacts.
Targeted secret scan found no committed secrets in the diff; matches were env-var names or existing audit text.
Targeted member-facing string scan found no em dashes in the added claim UI. The added copy is plain language.

`FOUNDING_MEMBER_LIMIT` remains the owner-set placeholder. I did not count it as a code blocker because the spec records it as a named pre-import owner precondition, but it must be set before a real import issues shareable certificates.

spec_compliance.criterion: 1. Import tooling validates and normalises rows, suppresses minors, imports idempotently, raises counter floor
spec_compliance.evidence: Minor suppression, batching, hashes, and counter floor exist, but duplicate emails are only skipped and legacy numbers are allowed to be absent, so validation does not enforce the claim conflict model or certificate requirement.

spec_compliance.criterion: 2. Claim detection post-login shows claim flow only for candidates and preserves no-member message otherwise
spec_compliance.evidence: Candidate and held states are handled, but `claim === undefined/null` falls through to the normal member dashboard.

spec_compliance.criterion: 3. Claim flow UI with DOB and three consents, pipeline hidden for minors
spec_compliance.evidence: DOB and consents are present, but the pipeline checkbox is always visible and submitted regardless of DOB/gender until backend response.

spec_compliance.criterion: 4. matchClaim keyed to auth email, safe outcomes, legacy carryover, consents, audit, idempotency
spec_compliance.evidence: It keys by auth email and handles DOB conflict/minor/idempotent member cases, but ambiguous duplicate-email rows can be claimed and ally pipeline consent can be stored true.

spec_compliance.criterion: 5. Migrated certificates carry legacy numbers and new signup counter cannot collide
spec_compliance.evidence: Counter floor logic exists, but legacy number is optional end to end and certificate issuance can fall back to a new counter number.

spec_compliance.criterion: 6. Suppressed minors are invisible to the wave
spec_compliance.evidence: Suppressed rows return only a held state unless file DOB now proves age-up, and `matchClaim` refuses self-declared minors without creating a member.

spec_compliance.criterion: 7. Imported-but-unclaimed rows never become active members
spec_compliance.evidence: Import writes only `importedMembers`; `members` rows are created only inside successful `matchClaim`.

Pure claim-lib tests exist, but the high-risk behavior added in this slice lacks Convex regression coverage for duplicate email conflict handling, non-standard pipeline consent, and legacy-number enforcement.
The diff introduces member-dashboard rendering for unlinked authenticated users and permits ally pipeline consent during claim, neither of which is in the claim-wave spec.

summary: Do not ship. The slice has real safeguarding and data-integrity regressions in the claim path, plus direct acceptance-criterion failures around duplicate-email conflicts, no-member handling, and legacy certificate numbers.
